ucs.title: [LLM] Machete (APT-C-43) PyInstaller-packed RAT extraction in %TEMP%\_MEI* (Pyark/Machete loader)
ucs.description: Hunts the defining artefact of Machete's espionage toolkit: a PyInstaller-bundled Python RAT (historically 'Pyark', 'Machete', 'Numpyrat') that, on execution, self-extracts its embedded Python runtime (python3X.dll, _ctypes.pyd, _socket.pyd, etc.) into a randomly numbered \Temp\_MEIxxxxx\ scratch directory. Machete has used PyInstaller exclusively as its packer since 2014 to compile the Spanish-language Python operator code into a single Windows EXE delivered to Latin American government/military targets — the _MEI extraction pattern, paired with a non-Microsoft initiating binary running from
ucs.splunk:
| tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_name) as dropped_files dc(Filesystem.file_name) as unique_drops from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created Filesystem.file_path="*\\Temp\\_MEI*" by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_guid Filesystem.user
| `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`
| where unique_drops >= 5
| rename process_guid as parent_proc_guid
| join type=inner dest parent_proc_guid [
    | tstats `summariesonly` values(Processes.process_name) as parent_name values(Processes.process_path) as parent_path values(Processes.process) as parent_cmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as grandparent values(Processes.process_hash) as parent_sha256 from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.dest Processes.process_guid
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
    | rename process_guid as parent_proc_guid
  ]
| where NOT match(parent_path, "(?i)C:\\\\(Windows|Program Files|Program Files \\(x86\\))\\\\")
| where match(parent_path, "(?i)\\\\(Users|ProgramData|AppData|Public|Temp|Downloads)\\\\")
| eval lure_chain=if(match(grandparent,"(?i)(winword|excel|powerpnt|outlook|wscript|cscript|mshta)\.exe"),"yes","no")
| table firstTime dest user parent_name parent_path parent_cmd grandparent parent_sha256 unique_drops dropped_files lure_chain
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
| sort - firstTime
ucs.kql:
// Machete (APT-C-43) — PyInstaller _MEI extraction by user-context loader
let PyInstallerHallmarks = dynamic(["python27.dll","python36.dll","python37.dll","python38.dll","python39.dll","python310.dll","python311.dll","_ctypes.pyd","_socket.pyd","_ssl.pyd","select.pyd","unicodedata.pyd","pyimod02_archive.pyc","pyimod03_importers.pyc","pyiboot01_bootstrap.pyc"]);
let OfficeLures = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","acrord32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","explorer.exe"]);
let MeiDrops = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FolderPath matches regex @"(?i)\\Temp\\_MEI\d{3,}\\"
    | where FileName in~ (PyInstallerHallmarks)
          or FileName endswith ".pyd"
          or (FileName startswith_cs "python" and FileName endswith ".dll");
MeiDrops
| summarize FirstDrop = min(Timestamp),
            LastDrop  = max(Timestamp),
            DroppedFiles = make_set(FileName, 50),
            DistinctDropped = dcount(FileName)
            by DeviceId, DeviceName,
               InitiatingProcessSHA256, InitiatingProcessFileName,
               InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
               InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessParentFileName
| where DistinctDropped >= 3                                  // PyInstaller drops 5-50 files; require >=3 to dodge legit single-DLL drops
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"            // exclude SYSTEM / machine-account installers
| where not(InitiatingProcessFolderPath startswith_cs @"C:\Windows\")
      and not(InitiatingProcessFolderPath startswith_cs @"C:\Program Files\")
      and not(InitiatingProcessFolderPath startswith_cs @"C:\Program Files (x86)\")
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\Users\", @"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Public\", @"\Temp\", @"\Downloads\")
| extend OfficeLureChain = iif(tolower(InitiatingProcessParentFileName) in (OfficeLures), "yes", "no")
| project FirstDrop, LastDrop, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          LoaderBinary = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          LoaderPath   = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          LoaderCmd    = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          LoaderSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          Grandparent  = InitiatingProcessParentFileName,
          OfficeLureChain, DistinctDropped, DroppedFiles
| order by FirstDrop desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Machete (APT-C-43) — PyInstaller _MEI extraction (ASIM port)
let PyInstallerHallmarks = dynamic(["python27.dll","python36.dll","python37.dll","python38.dll","python39.dll","python310.dll","python311.dll","_ctypes.pyd","_socket.pyd","_ssl.pyd","select.pyd","unicodedata.pyd","pyimod02_archive.pyc","pyimod03_importers.pyc","pyiboot01_bootstrap.pyc"]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where EventType == "FileCreated"
| where TargetFilePath matches regex @"(?i)\\Temp\\_MEI\d{3,}\\"
| where TargetFileName in~ (PyInstallerHallmarks)
      or TargetFileName endswith ".pyd"
      or (TargetFileName startswith_cs "python" and TargetFileName endswith ".dll")
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| summarize FirstDrop = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastDrop  = max(TimeGenerated),
            DroppedFiles = make_set(TargetFileName, 50),
            DistinctDropped = dcount(TargetFileName)
            by DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
               ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine
| where DistinctDropped >= 3
| where not(ActingProcessName has_any (@"C:\Windows\", @"C:\Program Files\"))
| order by FirstDrop desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Machete APT-C-43 PyInstaller _MEI Runtime Extraction
id: 5e6f3b27-4a9c-46d8-a4d5-8c1ae5b1d9ce
status: experimental
description: Detects creation of PyInstaller bootstrap files (python3X.dll, _ctypes.pyd, pyimod*.pyc) under %TEMP%\_MEIxxxxx\ — the unpacking pattern of Machete (APT-C-43) Spanish-language Python RATs (Pyark / Machete / Numpyrat).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0095/
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2019/08/05/machete-just-go-through/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.006
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1027.002
  - attack.g0095
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_path:
    TargetFilename|re: '(?i)\\Temp\\_MEI\d{3,}\\'
  selection_artefact:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\python27.dll'
      - '\python36.dll'
      - '\python37.dll'
      - '\python38.dll'
      - '\python39.dll'
      - '\python310.dll'
      - '\python311.dll'
      - '\_ctypes.pyd'
      - '\_socket.pyd'
      - '\_ssl.pyd'
      - '\pyimod02_archive.pyc'
      - '\pyimod03_importers.pyc'
      - '\pyiboot01_bootstrap.pyc'
  filter_loader_path:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Windows\'
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
  condition: selection_path and selection_artefact and not filter_loader_path
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate PyInstaller-packed enterprise tooling (Splunk UF wrappers, vendor agents) running from user paths — baseline by SHA256 of the loader
level: high
ucs.rationale: Machete is one of very few state-aligned actors whose entire offensive kit (publicly tracked since 2014 by Kaspersky, ESET, Cylance) is built in Python and shipped as PyInstaller-packed EXEs — when the loader fires, PyInstaller deterministically self-extracts a Python interpreter and stdlib .pyd modules into %TEMP%\_MEIxxxxx\. Generic 'suspicious python' rules miss this because Machete operators n

ucs.title: [LLM] Machete LatAm spearphish: Spanish-lure Office attachment → scripting host → schtasks persistence
ucs.description: Hunts Machete's end-to-end intrusion pattern observed against Venezuelan/Ecuadorian/Colombian government and military targets: an inbound Spanish-language email with an Office or RAR/SFX attachment is opened, the Office host (winword/excel/powerpnt) spawns a scripting interpreter (cscript/wscript/cmd/python), and within minutes a scheduled task is registered via schtasks.exe — Machete's documented persistence mechanism (T1053.005) for the Pyark/Machete RAT. The Spanish-keyword anchor in the lure plus the Office→script→schtasks chain on the same host is highly specific to APT-C-43.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as schtasks_time values(Processes.process) as schtasks_cmd values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.parent_process_name) as scripting_host from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=schtasks.exe (Processes.process="*/create*" OR Processes.process="*/CREATE*") Processes.parent_process_name IN (cmd.exe,wscript.exe,cscript.exe,powershell.exe,python.exe,pythonw.exe,mshta.exe) by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_guid
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| rename parent_process_guid as scripting_guid
| join type=inner dest scripting_guid [
    | tstats `summariesonly` min(_time) as script_time values(Processes.parent_process_name) as office_host values(Processes.process) as script_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN (winword.exe,excel.exe,powerpnt.exe,outlook.exe,acrord32.exe,wscript.exe,wmplayer.exe) by Processes.dest Processes.process_guid
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
    | rename process_guid as scripting_guid
  ]
| where (schtasks_time - script_time) < 600 AND schtasks_time >= script_time
| join type=left user [
    | tstats `summariesonly` values(All_Email.subject) as subject values(All_Email.src_user) as sender from datamodel=Email.All_Email where All_Email.action=delivered (All_Email.subject="*factura*" OR All_Email.subject="*comunicado*" OR All_Email.subject="*ministerio*" OR All_Email.subject="*ejercito*" OR All_Email.subject="*ej\xe9rcito*" OR All_Email.subject="*fuerza armada*" OR All_Email.subject="*denuncia*" OR All_Email.subject="*urgente*" OR All_Email.subject="*confidencial*" OR All_Email.subject="*citaci\xf3n*") by All_Email.recipient
    | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Email)`
    | rename recipient as user
  ]
| table schtasks_time dest user office_host scripting_host script_cmd schtasks_cmd subject sender
| sort - schtasks_time
ucs.kql:
// Machete (APT-C-43) — Spanish-lure phish → Office → script host → schtasks persistence chain
let WindowMin = 10m;
let OfficeLures   = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","acrord32.exe"]);
let ScriptHosts   = dynamic(["cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","python.exe","pythonw.exe"]);
let SpanishLureKw = dynamic(["factura","comunicado","ministerio","ejercito","ejército","fuerza armada","denuncia","urgente","confidencial","citación","citacion","presidencia","asamblea","informe","oficio"]);
// Stage 1: Spanish-language inbound phishing email with attachment
let PhishMail = EmailEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where EmailDirection == "Inbound"
          and DeliveryAction == "Delivered"
          and AttachmentCount >= 1
    | where (EmailLanguage in~ ("es","es-ES","es-MX","es-VE","es-CO","es-AR","pt-BR")
            or Subject has_any (SpanishLureKw))
    | project EmailTime = Timestamp, NetworkMessageId, Subject,
              SenderFromAddress, SenderFromDomain, RecipientEmailAddress;
// Stage 2: schtasks /create launched by a scripting host on the recipient's machine
let PersistChain = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/create","/Create","/CREATE")
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (ScriptHosts)
    | join kind=inner (
        DeviceProcessEvents
        | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
        | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeLures)
        | where FileName in~ (ScriptHosts)
        | project ScriptTime = Timestamp, DeviceId,
                  ScriptHost = FileName, ScriptCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
                  ScriptProcId = ProcessId, ScriptCreationTime = ProcessCreationTime,
                  OfficeHost = InitiatingProcessFileName,
                  OfficeCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
                  Recipient  = AccountUpn
      ) on DeviceId
              and $left.InitiatingProcessId == $right.ScriptProcId
              and $left.InitiatingProcessCreationTime == $right.ScriptCreationTime
    | where Timestamp between (ScriptTime .. ScriptTime + WindowMin)
    | project SchtasksTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountUpn,
              OfficeHost, OfficeCmd, ScriptHost, ScriptCmd,
              SchtasksCmd = ProcessCommandLine, Recipient;
// Stage 3: correlate to a Spanish-language phish to the same recipient within 24h prior
PersistChain
| join kind=leftouter PhishMail on $left.Recipient == $right.RecipientEmailAddress
| where isnull(EmailTime) or (SchtasksTime between (EmailTime .. EmailTime + 24h))
| project SchtasksTime, DeviceName, AccountUpn,
          OfficeHost, OfficeCmd, ScriptHost, ScriptCmd, SchtasksCmd,
          PhishSubject = Subject, PhishSender = SenderFromAddress, PhishTime = EmailTime
| order by SchtasksTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Machete (APT-C-43) — Office → script host → schtasks chain (Sentinel SecurityEvent + OfficeActivity)
let WindowMin = 10m;
let OfficeLures   = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","acrord32.exe"]);
let ScriptHosts   = dynamic(["cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","python.exe","pythonw.exe"]);
let SpanishLureKw = dynamic(["factura","comunicado","ministerio","ejercito","ejército","fuerza armada","denuncia","urgente","confidencial","citación","citacion","presidencia","asamblea","informe","oficio"]);
let ScriptFromOffice = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend ChildExe = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,"\\")[-1])),
             ParentExe = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName,"\\")[-1]))
    | where ParentExe in (OfficeLures) and ChildExe in (ScriptHosts)
    | project ScriptTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              OfficeHost = ParentExe, ScriptHost = ChildExe,
              ScriptPid = NewProcessId, ScriptCmd = CommandLine;
let SchtasksByScript = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend ChildExe = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,"\\")[-1])),
             ParentExe = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName,"\\")[-1]))
    | where ChildExe == "schtasks.exe"
    | where CommandLine has_any ("/create","/Create","/CREATE")
    | where ParentExe in (ScriptHosts)
    | project SchtasksTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              ParentPid = ProcessId, SchtasksCmd = CommandLine;
let Chain = ScriptFromOffice
    | join kind=inner SchtasksByScript on Computer, $left.ScriptPid == $right.ParentPid
    | where SchtasksTime between (ScriptTime .. ScriptTime + WindowMin);
let PhishMail = OfficeActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where OfficeWorkload =~ "Exchange"
    | where Operation in~ ("Deliver","MailItemsAccessed")
    | where Subject has_any (SpanishLureKw)
    | project PhishTime = TimeGenerated, Recipient = MailboxOwnerUPN,
              Subject, Sender;
Chain
| extend AccountUpn = tolower(strcat(tostring(split(Account,"\\")[-1]),"@",tostring(split(Account,"\\")[0])))
| join kind=leftouter PhishMail on $left.Account == $right.Recipient
| where isnull(PhishTime) or (SchtasksTime between (PhishTime .. PhishTime + 24h))
| project SchtasksTime, Computer, Account, OfficeHost, ScriptHost, ScriptCmd, SchtasksCmd, PhishSubject = Subject, PhishSender = Sender, PhishTime
| order by SchtasksTime desc
ucs.rationale: ESET ('Machete just got sharper', 2019) and Cylance ('El Machete's Malware Attacks Cut Through LATAM', 2017) both document the same intrusion pattern: Spanish-language phish → Office/RAR self-extractor → cscript/cmd dropper → `schtasks /create` registering the PyInstaller-packed Pyark/Machete RAT for persistence. Each stage on its own (Office spawning script, schtasks creation) is too noisy to ale
